channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: After 53 Years, This New Jersey Town’s Fight to Secede Is Over. Kind of.
item.description: South Seaside Park on the Jersey Shore has just succeeded in leaving Berkeley Township and joining its neighbor, Seaside Park, but there are still more challenges ahead.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Seaside Park, N.J.

item.title: Uber and Trial Lawyers Spar Over New Yorkers’ Auto Insurance Premiums
item.description: A powerful lawyers’ association is opposing a bill that would cap damages, but the ride-share company has spent millions lobbying state lawmakers to lower rates, some of the highest in the country.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes and Stefanos Chen
item.category: New York State Trial Lawyers Assn

media:content.media:credit: John Muggenborg for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The ride-share company Uber has become a deep-pocketed foe of the New York State Trial Lawyers Association, a trade group that has long wielded influence in state politics.

item.title: Cómo reducir tu exposición a los pesticidas
item.description: La ciencia no acaba de entender los riesgos para la salud de muchos productos químicos. Aquí te damos algunas sugerencias de cómo protegerte cuando preparas tus alimentos o fumigas contra plagas.
item.dc:creator: Allyson Chiu

item.title: ‘The Two of Them Looked at Each Other and Burst Into Laughter ’
item.description: Learning a memory trick at a bus stop, a lost neighborhood recalled and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 5, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Rescues Officer From Downed Fighter Jet in Iran, Trump Says
item.description: The rescue followed a two-day, life-or-death race between U.S. and Iranian forces to reach the injured airman, officials said.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Easter Reminds Us That Resurrection Is Everywhere
item.description: Marriages, careers, reputations, financial stability and dreams can all die. But that’s not the end of the story.
item.dc:creator: P.G. Sittenfeld

item.title: Women Need Treatment for Hot Flashes. Why Is This So Hard?
item.description: Hormone patches are in scarce supply because of increased demand — that’s mostly a good thing.
item.dc:creator: Gillian Goddard

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by Elizabeth Renstrom; source image by Picture Post/Stringer/Getty Images

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: China Built the World’s Drone Industry. Now It’s Locking Down the Skies.
item.description: The Chinese government tightened rules to curb what it described as illegal drone use, but some users say the changes are now restricting too many flights.
item.dc:creator: Joy Dong

media:content.media:description: A delivery drone last month above Shenzhen, China. China has sharply tightened rules for the use of recreational and civilian drones.

item.title: Trump prometió un final rápido a la guerra de Irán, pero no ha explicado cómo
item.description: El presidente Trump apostó a que la potencia de fuego estadounidense podría acobardar a Irán para que cumpliera las demandas de EE. UU. Hasta ahora, los líderes de Irán no han estado dispuestos a renunciar.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Ni el presidente Trump ni el secretario de Defensa, Pete Hegseth, han sido capaces de explicar cómo terminará la guerra, que ya va en su quinta semana.

item.title: U.S. Rescues Downed Air Force Officer Deep Inside Iran, Trump Says
item.description: An Air Force officer of a F-15E Strike Eagle shot down on Friday by Iran spent a day in hostile territory with little more than a pistol for protection.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper, Greg Jaffe and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: U.S. Air Force personnel last month at an air base in Britain.

item.title: Quote of the Day: How a Man, His Brother and A.I. Built a $1.8 Billion Company
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, April 5, 2026.

item.title: Hollywood Writers Union and Studios Reach Contract Deal
item.description: With the global entertainment business reeling during a period of rapid change, there was little enthusiasm on either side for a costly standoff.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

media:content.media:credit: Jenna Schoenefeld for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the Writers Guild of America on striking in 2023. The contract talks then were particularly drawn out and acrimonious.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: The U.S. military’s search-and-rescue operation for a missing American airman entered its second day. The Israeli military struck a major petrochemical complex in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The site of a strike at Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Judge Pauses Trump Demand for Student Race Data in 17 States
item.description: The Trump administration had said it would collect data from colleges to ensure compliance with a Supreme Court ruling ending affirmative action in admissions.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:description: Linda McMahon, the education secretary, last year.

item.title: Israel Strikes Iran’s Largest Petrochemical Complex
item.description: The sprawling complex in the city of Mahshahr was taken offline after strikes hit two utility plants that provided basic services like gas and power, Iranian officials said.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The Mahshahr petrochemical complex in 2011.

item.title: What Is It Like to Get Cancer When You're Young?
item.description: Hundreds of New York Times readers shared their experiences with early-onset cancers. Here are seven of their stories.
item.dc:creator: Nina Agrawal

media:content.media:credit: Leah Nash, Chet Strange, Jared Soares, Dustin Chambers and Justin T. Gellerson for The New York Times

item.title: Dozens Killed in Haiti Massacre as International Force Trickles In
item.description: Gangs tore through several rural communities last weekend, underscoring the challenges that will face the new, U.N.-backed Gang Suppression Force starting to enter the country.
item.dc:creator: André Paultre and Frances Robles

item.title: Badvertising
item.description: Samuel A. Donaldson brandishes a sharp Sunday puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: George Freston/Hulton Archive, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Our extra large printers provide home security!

item.title: At Least 15 Injured After Driver Plows Into a Louisiana Festival
item.description: Some of the pedestrians at the parade in New Iberia, La., were critically hurt, according to the authorities, who said the driver was impaired after the crash.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler and Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: Emily Kask for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The downtown in New Iberia, La., in 2023. The driver of a vehicle who plowed into a crowd of people at a parade was taken into custody on Saturday, officials said.

item.title: Where Are NASA’s Artemis II Astronauts Now? Closer to the Moon Than Earth.
item.description: The astronauts said they had lost track of which day it is on Earth on their transit to the moon.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

media:content.media:description: In a photo taken by a crew member, the moon is seen from a window on the Orion spacecraft Integrity during the Artemis II mission on Friday.

item.title: Mary Rand, Pioneering Track and Field Olympian, Dies at 86
item.description: At the 1964 Tokyo Olympics, she became the first British woman to win a gold medal in track and field, and the first to win three medals in one Games.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

media:content.media:description: Rand in 1969. She embodied the freedom, optimism and mod fashion of the Swinging Sixties in England.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Tom Valenti, Who Made Lamb Shanks a Showstopper, Dies at 67
item.description: At Ouest, on the Upper West Side, and other Manhattan restaurants, he served robust dishes in a style he called “haute cuisine with the grandma gene.”
item.dc:creator: Pete Wells

media:content.media:credit: Don Hogan Charles/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tom Valenti cooking in 2003. After the Sept. 11 attacks, he organized a fund-raising effort to help the families of the food and beverage workers who had been killed at the World Trade Center.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Deadly Earthquake and Floods Worsen Afghanistan’s Troubles
item.description: Floods have killed at least 77 people this week and destroyed or damaged thousands of homes, and an earthquake on Friday killed a dozen more.
item.dc:creator: Yaqoob Akbary
item.category: United States Agency for International Development

media:content.media:description: Afghans beside a flooded road in Herat Province on Thursday.

item.title: El papa León discrepa con el secretario de Defensa de EE. UU.
item.description: En un agudo contraste con los llamamientos del gobierno de Trump a las oraciones cristianas por el esfuerzo de guerra, el papa León XIV dice que la dominación militar es “totalmente ajena al camino de Jesucristo”.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: El papa León, durante la misa del Jueves Santo en Roma, dijo que la misión cristiana había sido a menudo “trastocada por lógicas de dominio”.

item.title: El aviador desaparecido suscita preocupaciones de que Irán obtenga una ventaja sobre EE. UU.
item.description: Desde 1979, Irán ha utilizado en repetidas ocasiones a estadounidenses y europeos detenidos en su territorio para obtener concesiones frente a adversarios más poderosos.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dos mujeres caminan junto a un mural de la Estatua de la Libertad en el muro exterior de la antigua embajada estadounidense en Teherán. En 1979, un grupo de estudiantes militantes tomó la embajada y mantuvo cautivos a 52 estadounidenses durante 444 días.

item.title: Some Voters Say Congress Is Too Old. These Black Democrats Aren’t Leaving.
item.description: As older members of Congress head for the exits amid growing pressure for fresh faces in the Democratic Party, some of the most seasoned Black lawmakers are resisting retirement.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison

media:content.media:description: Representative Al Green, Democrat of Texas, made headlines last year for shaking his cane at President Trump. Mr. Green, 78, is seeking re-election to Congress.

item.title: Trump quería algo que Pam Bondi no le pudo dar
item.description: El verdadero motivo del descontento de Trump con la fiscala general era, al parecer, su incapacidad para satisfacer su necesidad de venganza contra sus enemigos.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Toobin
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates

item.title: What Teens Are Doing With Those Role-Playing Chatbots
item.description: Harassing bots with “funny violence.” Confiding about a broken heart. Chatting with a block of cheese. Filling a void of loneliness.
item.dc:creator: Kashmir Hill and Frances F. Denny

media:content.media:description: Quentin first downloaded a character chatbot when he was 13. He remembers thinking, “Wow, this is garbage, but fun.”

item.title: U.S. Fighter Jet Downed Over Iran Was Probably Based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, U.K. Airfield
item.description: A military analyst identified markings consistent with a squadron based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, one of two British bases that host the largest U.S. fighter jet operation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: A fighter jet at R.A.F. Lakenheath in Mildenhall, England, in January.

item.title: How to Make Perfect, Golden Chicken Schnitzel
item.description: Sue Li shares tips for a puffy, airy crust and pairs her schnitzel with a fresh cucumber salad full of parsley and dill.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Cyd Raftus McDowell.
media:content.media:description: Sue Li’s chicken schnitzel with cucumber salad.

item.title: Nadie me había tocado en cuatro años. Escribiendo, volví a la vida
item.description: Era 14 años menor que yo, y ‘skater’. ¿Por qué no invitarlo a pasar?
item.dc:creator: Angela Cravens

item.title: Orion Samuelson, the Elvis Presley of Agricultural Radio, Dies at 91
item.description: As a host at WGN in Chicago for 60 years, he shared news that was essential to farmers in a homespun style that appealed to listeners from the city.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald

item.title: Easy, Breezy Springtime Desserts
item.description: It’s time for simple showstoppers like toasted coconut rum balls and blueberry, lemon and almond cake.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Mark Weinberg for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Yossy Arefi.
media:content.media:description: Scott Loitsch’s Rice Krispies treats.

item.title: Lights, Camera, Crafty: The Woman Who Feeds the Movie Crews
item.description: Watch Concetta Scottodicarlo in action, as she feeds the cast and crew of a film production.
item.dc:creator: Priya Krishna

media:content.media:credit: Jia Li for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Concetta Scottodicarlo has been working on film crews for more than a decade.

item.title: 4 consejos para animarte a escribir cartas a mano
item.description: No necesitas una caligrafía perfecta. Aquí tienes algunas formas de crear el hábito.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Passarella

item.title: Trump Slashed Science Funding. Now the U.S. Could Face a Costly Brain Drain.
item.description: The White House’s attacks on academia and budget cuts for research have provided an opening for other countries to poach leading scientists.
item.dc:creator: Vivienne Walt

media:content.media:credit: David Payr for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Wali Malik, a robotics engineer, left his job in Massachusetts last year to lead a new lab in Vienna.

item.title: Memories of the ‘Last, Best Time to Be a Kid’
item.description: Readers react to Sarah Wildman’s guest essay about the free-range childhood depicted in the Rob Reiner film “Stand by Me.”

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; photographs by Columbia Pictures and Ackun/Getty Images

item.title: Three Charged With Arson in Attack on U.K. Jewish Charity’s Ambulances
item.description: A fourth suspect was arrested after showing up at a hearing for the defendants, the police said. The March attack was widely condemned but has not been declared a terrorist incident.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:description: The aftermath of the March 23 arson attack, which took place outside a synagogue in north London.

item.title: Pam and Kristi, Kicked to the Curb
item.description: Trump girls gone wild — or just gone.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Dowd

item.title: Trump’s Relentless, ‘Utterly Incoherent’ Battles
item.description: A look at a conflict without end and a constitution under pressure.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle, Jamelle Bouie, David French and Derek Arthur
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; photograph by Alex Brandon/Getty

item.title: Trump Needs Smarter Sycophants
item.description: Loyal losers don’t go very far with this president.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: I Was Broadway’s First Grizabella. I Couldn’t Have Imagined the New ‘Cats.’
item.description: A new, queer reimagining of “Cats” showed me something about the show I never expected.
item.dc:creator: Betty Buckley

item.title: U.S. Rescues Downed Air Force Officer Deep Inside Iran, Trump Says
item.description: American commandos carried out a risky mission in Iran to extract an injured crew member who bailed out when a U.S. fighter jet was shot down on Friday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
